channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Call for Applications: Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Monitoring Mental Health Impacts of Climate Change (Up to £100,000 Funding)
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change has officially announced a competitive and highly significant call for applications for the Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Mental Health and Climate Change. This initiative is designed to support the development of a scientifically rigorous indicator that will track and monitor the mental health impacts […]

The post Call for Applications: Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Monitoring Mental Health Impacts of Climate Change (Up to £100,000 Funding) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Lancet Countdown on Health and Climate Change has officially announced a competitive and highly significant call for applications for the Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Mental Health and Climate Change. This initiative is designed to support the development of a scientifically rigorous indicator that will track and monitor the mental health impacts of climate change-related hazards globally.

This award is established in honour of Professor Tord Envold Kjellström (1948–2025), a globally respected environmental and occupational health scientist whose pioneering research significantly advanced understanding of climate-related health risks, including occupational heat exposure and environmental change impacts.

The selected expert or expert team will collaborate closely with the Lancet Countdown team to design an indicator that meets strict scientific criteria and contributes to global climate-health monitoring systems.

Objective of the Award

The primary goal of this opportunity is to develop a robust and validated indicator capable of measuring how climate change influences mental health outcomes. This indicator will become part of the Lancet Countdown’s annual monitoring framework, contributing to global policy and scientific understanding.

The work will focus on:

  • Developing a scientifically valid mental health–climate change indicator
  • Ensuring methodological rigor aligned with Lancet Countdown indicator standards
  • Collaborating with global experts in climate health monitoring
  • Supporting integration into annual global reporting systems
  • Advancing evidence-based climate and mental health policy

Funding Details

This award provides substantial research support for the selected applicant(s):

  • Funding amount: Up to £100,000 per award
  • Funding duration: 6 to 18 months
  • Number of awards: One
  • Co-applicants: Eligible
  • Funding structure: One-off award
  • Host requirements: Must be eligible to receive funding from Wellcome

Key Dates and Timeline

Applicants must carefully note the official timeline:

  • Applications open: 1 June 2026 at 08:00 BST
  • Application deadline: 12 July 2026 at 17:00 BST
  • Duration of project: 6–18 months following award allocation

Late submissions will not be considered.

Application Process

Interested applicants are expected to:

  1. Review the full Guidance for Applicants provided by Lancet Countdown
  2. Download and complete the official application pack
  3. Submit applications via the designated Qualtrics submission platform once applications open
  4. Participate in or review the information webinar for further clarification on requirements

Applicants are encouraged to ensure that their proposals align closely with the Lancet Countdown’s scientific framework and indicator methodology.

For further inquiries, applicants may contact:

  • Email: info@lancetcountdown.org

Expected Outputs

Successful applicants will be responsible for producing:

  • A validated mental health and climate change indicator
  • Scientific documentation supporting methodology and implementation
  • Collaboration outputs with Lancet Countdown research teams
  • Contribution to global annual climate-health reporting

Importance of This Award

Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major driver of mental health challenges, including anxiety, trauma, displacement stress, and disaster-related psychological impacts. However, global standardized indicators to measure these effects remain limited.

This award addresses that gap by supporting innovation in:

  • Climate-sensitive mental health surveillance
  • Global health indicator development
  • Policy-relevant climate-health data systems
  • Evidence-based climate resilience strategies

Eligibility Summary (Separate Section)

Who Can Apply

  • Individual experts or expert teams
  • Researchers in mental health, epidemiology, climate science, public health, or related fields
  • Applicants with strong experience in indicator development or health metrics
  • Institutions capable of receiving Wellcome funding

Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to international applicants
  • Applicants from all countries and continents may apply, provided institutional eligibility for funding is met

Required Expertise Areas

  • Mental health research
  • Climate change and environmental health
  • Epidemiology or biostatistics
  • Public health indicators and data systems
  • Health systems research

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Award Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Call for Applications: Professor Tord Kjellström Indicator Development Award for Monitoring Mental Health Impacts of Climate Change (Up to £100,000 Funding) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme 2026: Fully Supported Cybersecurity Capacity Building Opportunity for African AI Startups
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications Open for the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme African artificial intelligence startups now have a unique opportunity to strengthen their cybersecurity capabilities, improve operational resilience, and prepare for sustainable growth through the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme. The Cyber4Africa Programme is a collaborative initiative delivered by the AI Hub for […]

The post AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme 2026: Fully Supported Cybersecurity Capacity Building Opportunity for African AI Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme

African artificial intelligence startups now have a unique opportunity to strengthen their cybersecurity capabilities, improve operational resilience, and prepare for sustainable growth through the AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme.

The Cyber4Africa Programme is a collaborative initiative delivered by the AI Hub for Sustainable Development, Cyber 4.0, and Cisco Kenya. Designed specifically for Africa’s growing AI ecosystem, the programme aims to equip startups with the cybersecurity knowledge, technical support, and operational tools required to scale securely in increasingly complex digital environments.

As artificial intelligence solutions become more deeply integrated into critical sectors such as healthcare, financial services, government technology, energy systems, and digital infrastructure, cybersecurity has become a fundamental requirement for sustainable innovation. The Cyber4Africa Programme addresses this need by helping startups build security and trust into their products and operations from the beginning.

About the AI Hub for Sustainable Development

The AI Hub for Sustainable Development is a G7-endorsed initiative jointly led by the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy and the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) under the Italy-Africa Mattei Plan.

The Hub was established to foster transformative AI innovation across Africa by supporting entrepreneurs, innovators, startups, and ecosystem builders. Working with more than 130 innovators across 18 countries, the initiative seeks to accelerate AI-driven development and create solutions that address some of the continent’s most pressing challenges.

The Hub focuses on strengthening three key pillars:

  • Green compute infrastructure
  • Data ecosystems
  • Talent development

Through strategic partnerships involving African countries, Italy, the European Union, and G7 stakeholders, the Hub is creating an ecosystem where innovators can access resources, partnerships, and opportunities needed to build impactful AI solutions.

What is the Cyber4Africa Programme?

Cyber4Africa extends the broader AI Hub ecosystem by focusing specifically on cybersecurity and digital trust.

The programme recognizes that many promising AI startups face growing security challenges as they scale. These challenges include:

  • Data protection vulnerabilities
  • Cybersecurity threats
  • Infrastructure security risks
  • Regulatory compliance requirements
  • Operational security weaknesses
  • Trust and safety concerns within AI systems

To address these issues, Cyber4Africa helps startups integrate cybersecurity principles throughout the AI development lifecycle. The programme promotes a “safety-by-design” approach that embeds security considerations into products, platforms, and services from the earliest stages of development.

By participating in the programme, startups can strengthen their resilience while increasing investor confidence, customer trust, and readiness for expansion into new markets.

Programme Objectives

The Cyber4Africa Programme seeks to:

  1. Improve cybersecurity awareness among African AI startups.
  2. Strengthen digital resilience across emerging AI businesses.
  3. Enhance data protection and privacy practices.
  4. Support secure infrastructure development.
  5. Enable safer deployment of AI technologies.
  6. Foster sustainable growth within Africa’s AI ecosystem.
  7. Build trust and security as foundational elements of innovation.

Key Benefits for Selected Startups

Selected participants will gain access to structured support designed to improve both technical and organizational cybersecurity readiness.

Benefits include:

1. Cyber Resilience and Awareness

Participants will receive support aimed at strengthening organizational cybersecurity awareness and preparedness.

Areas covered may include:

  • Cyber risk identification
  • Security best practices
  • Threat awareness
  • Incident preparedness
  • Security governance

2. Training and Capacity Building

Startups will benefit from specialized cybersecurity training designed to improve internal capabilities and strengthen long-term resilience.

Training may help teams:

  • Understand emerging cyber threats
  • Improve security management practices
  • Build secure AI systems
  • Enhance operational security procedures
  • Develop stronger compliance frameworks

3. Technical and Operational Support

The programme provides practical assistance to help startups address cybersecurity challenges affecting their products and operations.

Support may include:

  • Security assessments
  • Technical guidance
  • Infrastructure security recommendations
  • Data protection support
  • Operational resilience strategies

4. Access to Industry Expertise

Participants become part of a wider ecosystem involving:

  • AI Hub partners
  • Cybersecurity experts
  • Industry leaders
  • Innovation ecosystem stakeholders
  • Public and private sector collaborators

5. Specialized Training in Kenya

A unique benefit is available to Kenya-based innovators.

Eligible startups from Kenya will receive access to on-site training opportunities at the Cisco Cybersecurity Training Centre located in Nairobi, Kenya.

This provides hands-on learning experiences and exposure to industry-standard cybersecurity practices.

Priority Sectors

While startups from various sectors may apply, priority consideration is given to organizations operating in:

  • Healthcare
  • Financial Technology (Fintech)
  • Government Technology (GovTech)
  • Energy
  • AI Infrastructure

These sectors are particularly important because of their growing reliance on AI systems and their increased exposure to cybersecurity risks.

Why Cybersecurity Matters for AI Startups

Artificial intelligence solutions increasingly process large amounts of sensitive data and support critical decision-making processes.

Without adequate cybersecurity protections, startups may face:

  • Data breaches
  • Financial losses
  • Regulatory penalties
  • Service disruptions
  • Reputational damage
  • Loss of customer trust

The Cyber4Africa Programme helps startups proactively address these risks while positioning themselves for sustainable and secure growth.

Countries Represented in the Programme Ecosystem

The application process specifically recognizes startups operating across numerous African countries, including:

  • Algeria
  • Angola
  • Côte d’Ivoire
  • Democratic Republic of Congo
  • Egypt
  • Ethiopia
  • Gabon
  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Mauritania
  • Morocco
  • Mozambique
  • Republic of Congo
  • Rwanda
  • Senegal
  • Tanzania
  • Tunisia
  • Zambia

Startups operating in other African countries are also encouraged to apply where eligible.

Application Deadline

Application Deadline: 8 June 2026 at 23:59 Central European Time (CET).

Interested startups should submit their applications before the deadline to ensure consideration.

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

Africa’s AI ecosystem is expanding rapidly, creating exciting opportunities for innovators developing solutions that can transform industries and improve lives. However, sustainable growth requires strong cybersecurity foundations.

The AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme provides African AI startups with access to training, technical support, resilience-building resources, and expert guidance needed to strengthen cybersecurity readiness and scale confidently.

For startups operating in sectors where data protection, trust, and digital resilience are essential, this programme offers an opportunity to build stronger systems, safer products, and more sustainable pathways for growth.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post AI Hub for Sustainable Development Cyber4Africa Programme 2026: Fully Supported Cybersecurity Capacity Building Opportunity for African AI Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Top Artificial Intelligence Safety, Governance, Security, and Policy Fellowships Open for Applications in 2026
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming societies, economies, governments, and global security systems. As AI capabilities continue to advance, there is growing demand for professionals who can help shape AI governance, strengthen AI safety, improve AI security, and ensure responsible deployment of powerful AI systems. Several prestigious fellowships and leadership programs are currently accepting applications for […]

The post Top Artificial Intelligence Safety, Governance, Security, and Policy Fellowships Open for Applications in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming societies, economies, governments, and global security systems. As AI capabilities continue to advance, there is growing demand for professionals who can help shape AI governance, strengthen AI safety, improve AI security, and ensure responsible deployment of powerful AI systems.

Several prestigious fellowships and leadership programs are currently accepting applications for 2026 cohorts. These opportunities provide funding, mentorship, networking, research support, career development, and direct engagement with leading experts working at the forefront of artificial intelligence policy, governance, security, and safety.

This article highlights some of the most significant opportunities currently available to professionals, researchers, students, engineers, policymakers, and emerging leaders seeking to contribute to the future of AI.

1. Talos Network Artificial Intelligence Policy Leaders Programme 2026

The Talos Network Artificial Intelligence Policy Leaders Programme is designed for experienced professionals who are ready to influence the future of AI governance in Europe.

The programme aims to strengthen Europe’s capacity to govern advanced AI systems safely and effectively by supporting professionals with extensive experience in policy, government, international organizations, academia, industry, and related sectors.

Programme Tracks

Placement Track

The Placement Track is intended for professionals with at least eight years of experience who want to transition into AI governance.

Participants receive:

  • A 12-month paid placement at an AI-focused organization
  • Base compensation of EUR 5,000 per month for Brussels-based placements
  • Cost-of-living adjustments for placements in other cities
  • Career transition support
  • Mentorship and networking opportunities

Training Track

The Training Track targets senior professionals with at least twelve years of experience who want to incorporate AI governance and AI safety considerations into their current roles.

Participants receive:

  • Advanced AI governance training
  • Strategic positioning support
  • Access to high-level policy networks
  • Career coaching and mentorship

Programme Components

Virtual European AI Policy Bootcamp

  • Dates: 10 September – 22 October 2026
  • Weekly sessions every Thursday
  • Approximately 10 hours per week
  • Compatible with full-time employment

Policymaking Summit

  • Dates: 3 October – 9 October 2026
  • Location: Brussels
  • Fully reimbursed participation
  • Workshops, networking, and engagement with senior policymakers

Additional Benefits

  • One-on-one mentoring
  • Career coaching
  • Conference funding support
  • Coworking support
  • Access to Talos Network alumni
  • Strategic career planning
  • Connections across government, think tanks, industry, and European institutions

Who Should Consider Applying?

  • Government professionals
  • Policy experts
  • Think tank researchers
  • Industry professionals
  • Academics
  • Professionals interested in AI governance careers

APPLY HERE

2. Black in Artificial Intelligence Safety and Ethics Fellowship Fall 2026

The Black in Artificial Intelligence Safety and Ethics Fellowship is a 13-week remote programme designed to support Black researchers, practitioners, and future leaders working in AI safety, AI security, and AI governance.

Programme Dates

  • Start Date: 8 September 2026
  • End Date: 18 December 2026

Research Tracks

Participants may specialize in:

AI Alignment

Areas include:

  • Interpretability
  • Oversight and control
  • Agency research
  • Machine learning safety

AI Security

Areas include:

  • Cybersecurity
  • Adversarial robustness
  • Risk management
  • Threat detection

AI Governance

Areas include:

  • Policy frameworks
  • Standards
  • Evaluations
  • Systemic risk analysis

Programme Structure

Phase One: Foundations and Training

Weeks 1–5 focus on:

  • AI safety seminars
  • Expert-led training
  • Structured curriculum
  • Technical and analytical skills development

Phase Two: Research and Project Development

Weeks 6–13 focus on:

  • Mentor-guided research
  • Weekly supervision
  • Capstone project development

Potential Capstone Outputs

  • Research preprints
  • Technical articles
  • Fellowship applications
  • Grant proposals

Benefits

  • Expert mentorship
  • AI safety training
  • Research experience
  • Professional development
  • Access to AI safety networks
  • Career preparation opportunities

Deadline

Applications are currently open. Applicants should monitor the official programme page for closing dates and updates.

APPLY HERE

3. Secure Program Synthesis Fellowship 2026

The Secure Program Synthesis Fellowship is a collaborative initiative led by Apart Research and Atlas Computing.

The fellowship focuses on one of the most important emerging challenges in AI safety: ensuring that AI-generated software behaves as intended and remains secure.

Programme Dates

  • June – September 2026

Focus Areas

Participants may work on projects involving:

Specification Elicitation

Developing methods for translating informal requirements into formal specifications.

Specification Validation

Creating tools that verify the accuracy and completeness of specifications.

Specification-Driven Development

Building systems that generate and evaluate implementations based on formal specifications.

Adversarial Robustness

Exploring weaknesses and failure modes in AI-assisted software development systems.

Benefits

  • Research mentorship
  • Compute resources
  • API support
  • Community engagement
  • Travel funding
  • Demo Day participation
  • Publication opportunities
  • Research collaboration

Commitment

  • 8–30 hours per week
  • Fully remote

Deadline

The participant application deadline was 14 June 2026.

APPLY HERE

4. Stanford University Center for International Security and Cooperation Artificial Intelligence Fellowships 2026–2027

Stanford University’s Center for International Security and Cooperation offers prestigious fellowship opportunities focused on AI, international security, geopolitics, and strategic competition.

Open Fellowship Tracks

  • Artificial Intelligence in Strategic Stability and Military Competition Fellowship
  • Geopolitics of Artificial Intelligence Fellowship

Benefits

  • One academic year of research
  • Faculty mentorship
  • Access to Stanford researchers
  • Participation in seminars
  • Collaborative research environment
  • Professional development opportunities

Deadline

Applications close on 8 June 2026.

Ideal Candidates

  • Researchers
  • Scholars
  • Security experts
  • AI governance professionals
  • International affairs specialists

APPLY HERE

5. Constellation Visiting Fellowship Fall 2026

The Constellation Visiting Fellowship supports AI safety researchers through an immersive research residency in Berkeley, California.

Duration

  • Three to six months

Benefits

Housing and Travel

  • Fully covered housing
  • Travel support

Workspace

  • 24/7 office access
  • Collaborative research environment

Meals

  • Lunch and dinner provided on weekdays

Networking

  • Access to more than 100 researchers
  • Research talks and events

Visa Support

  • Guidance for international participants

Program Requirements

Applicants should be working full-time on AI safety or closely related research.

Deadline

Applications close on 12 June 2026 at 11:59 PM Anywhere on Earth.

APPLY HERE

6. Coefficient Giving Navigators Incubator – Artificial Intelligence Security Cohort

The Navigators Incubator is a leadership development programme focused on solving neglected and high-impact global catastrophic risk challenges.

The first cohort focuses on securing transformative artificial intelligence.

What Participants Receive

  • Salary matching support
  • Research funding
  • Compute resources
  • Conference budgets
  • Networking opportunities
  • Advisory support
  • Potential long-term project funding

Program Duration

  • Six months to two years

Focus Areas

Participants may work on:

  • AI model security
  • Infrastructure protection
  • Compute verification
  • AI integrity
  • AI control
  • Rogue deployment detection

Locations

  • Washington, D.C.
  • San Francisco
  • London
  • Tel Aviv
  • Remote participation available

Ideal Candidates

  • Cybersecurity professionals
  • National security experts
  • AI security researchers
  • Technical founders
  • Security engineers

Deadline

Applications are currently open. Interested candidates should apply as early as possible.

APPLY HERE

7. Center for Human-Compatible Artificial Intelligence Research Fellowship

The Center for Human-Compatible Artificial Intelligence (CHAI) at the University of California, Berkeley, offers a prestigious research fellowship designed to train outstanding postdoctoral researchers working to advance beneficial and human-compatible artificial intelligence. Fellows work closely with leading AI researchers, including Stuart Russell, Pieter Abbeel, and Anca Dragan, while collaborating with Berkeley AI Research and affiliated institutions.

About the Fellowship

The fellowship is intended for highly qualified researchers interested in developing AI systems that remain aligned with human values and interests. Fellows have significant freedom to pursue original research across a wide range of AI-related disciplines.

Research Areas

Potential research topics include:

  • Theoretical foundations of intelligent agent architecture
  • Probabilistic programming and formal probability models
  • Long-term decision making
  • Lifelong learning systems
  • Human-in-the-loop planning and reinforcement learning
  • Human-robot interaction
  • Human preference modeling
  • Multi-human value alignment
  • Robust cooperation in multi-agent systems
  • Mechanism design for human-machine systems

Fellowship Benefits

Participants receive:

  • Funding for two years, with the possibility of renewal up to three years based on performance
  • Competitive salary based on University of California, Berkeley salary scales
  • Visa sponsorship eligibility
  • Visa cost support for fellows and eligible dependents
  • Access to world-leading AI researchers and research networks
  • Opportunities to supervise students and lead research collaborations

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold or be close to completing a PhD in computer science, statistics, mathematics, theoretical economics, or a related technical discipline
  • Demonstrate a strong publication and research record
  • Have a solid understanding of modern AI and statistical learning methods (preferred)

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  1. Resume/Curriculum Vitae
  2. One-page statement of research interests
  3. Contact details for two academic referees

Deadline

No application deadline is specified. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

APPLY HERE

Why These Opportunities Matter

The future of artificial intelligence will be shaped by the people who understand both its capabilities and its risks. These fellowships and leadership programmes provide unique opportunities to gain specialized expertise, build influential networks, contribute to impactful research, and help shape policies governing some of the most consequential technologies of our time.

Whether your background is in policy, engineering, cybersecurity, governance, academia, or international affairs, these programmes provide pathways to become part of the growing global ecosystem working to ensure advanced AI systems remain safe, secure, and beneficial.

Given the rapidly approaching deadlines for several opportunities, interested applicants should prepare their materials and submit applications as soon as possible.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Top Artificial Intelligence Safety, Governance, Security, and Policy Fellowships Open for Applications in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 in Brussels, Belgium | 12-Month European Policy Internship
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Microsoft has officially opened applications for its highly prestigious Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 based in Brussels, Belgium. This exciting opportunity is designed for students and early-career professionals who are passionate about European politics, technology policy, cloud infrastructure, digital sovereignty, and international governance. The internship will begin in August or September 2026 and will run […]

The post Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 in Brussels, Belgium | 12-Month European Policy Internship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft has officially opened applications for its highly prestigious Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 based in Brussels, Belgium. This exciting opportunity is designed for students and early-career professionals who are passionate about European politics, technology policy, cloud infrastructure, digital sovereignty, and international governance.

The internship will begin in August or September 2026 and will run for a duration of 12 months, offering selected candidates the chance to work directly with Microsoft’s European Government Affairs team in Brussels.

This internship is an exceptional opportunity for aspiring policy professionals, public affairs specialists, international relations graduates, and technology policy enthusiasts who want to gain hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading technology companies.

About the Microsoft Government Affairs Internship 2026

Microsoft is recruiting a Cloud, Infrastructure & Tech Sovereignty Policy Coordinator Intern to support its European Government Affairs Office in Brussels. The role will involve working closely with senior government affairs professionals and contributing to Microsoft’s engagement with European institutions and stakeholders.

The internship is supervised under the leadership of Nanna-Louise Linde, Microsoft’s Vice President for European Government Affairs.

Microsoft’s European Government Affairs organization focuses on strengthening relationships and dialogue with European policymakers and institutions while supporting Europe’s broader digital transformation agenda.

Interns will gain direct exposure to:

  • European Union policymaking
  • Cloud and AI governance
  • Technology sovereignty discussions
  • Digital infrastructure policy
  • Telecom and subsea cable policy
  • Quantum computing policy
  • Corporate public affairs strategy
  • Executive stakeholder engagement

This is a high-impact internship where interns will actively contribute to policy analysis, stakeholder coordination, strategic planning, event management, and executive engagement preparation.

Internship Location

  • Brussels Region, Belgium

Internship Duration

  • 12 Months

Internship Start Date

  • August 2026 or September 2026

Work Model

  • Hybrid work arrangement
  • 3 days per week in-office

Employment Type

  • Internship

Professional Area

  • Corporate Affairs
  • Government Affairs

Travel Requirements

  • Less than 25%

Key Responsibilities

Selected interns will support Microsoft’s European Government Affairs Office through a broad range of responsibilities related to policy coordination, stakeholder engagement, and strategic communications.

1. Political, Policy, and Regulatory Analysis

Interns will:

  • Monitor emerging European technology policy developments
  • Analyze public policy issues related to cloud infrastructure and AI
  • Support policy research on sovereign cloud technologies
  • Study developments in telecom and subsea cable infrastructure
  • Track discussions on quantum computing policy
  • Coordinate contributions from internal stakeholders
  • Prepare summaries and policy analyses for internal teams

2. Strategic Planning and Stakeholder Engagement

Interns will:

  • Conduct stakeholder mapping exercises
  • Build engagement strategies with EU institutions
  • Support outreach to policymakers and regulators
  • Help identify collaboration opportunities across Europe
  • Assist in planning government affairs initiatives
  • Contribute to internal policy strategy development

3. Project Management and Event Coordination

Participants will:

  • Assist in organizing high-level political engagements
  • Coordinate executive visits and meetings in Brussels
  • Support logistics for conferences and policy events
  • Work with European institutions, think tanks, and trade associations
  • Help manage cross-functional event planning activities

4. Research and Writing

Interns will:

  • Draft briefing notes and policy documents
  • Support campaign-related research
  • Develop executive talking points
  • Assist with conference preparation materials
  • Edit presentations and strategic communication documents
  • Prepare internal policy decks and engagement materials

5. External Representation

Interns may also:

  • Attend external policy events and meetings
  • Represent Microsoft in relevant discussions
  • Provide detailed event summaries and analysis reports

Qualifications and Eligibility Requirements

Microsoft is seeking highly motivated and analytical candidates with a strong interest in technology policy and European governance.

Applicants should possess:

Educational Background

A degree or academic background in:

  • Law
  • Political Science
  • International Relations
  • Philosophy
  • Social Sciences
  • Related disciplines

Preferred Experience

Previous experience through:

  • Internships
  • Public affairs consultancies
  • EU institutions
  • Policy organizations
  • Corporate affairs teams
  • Government-related environments

Required Skills

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Excellent analytical abilities
  • Strong communication skills
  • Project management experience
  • Organizational and planning skills
  • Attention to detail
  • Networking abilities
  • Team collaboration skills
  • Adaptability in fast-paced environments

Additional Requirements

Applicants should also have:

  • Strong interest in European and international politics
  • Passion for technology and digital policy
  • Fluency in spoken and written English
  • Ability to manage competing deadlines
  • Positive and collaborative mindset

Knowledge of additional European languages will be considered an advantage.

Benefits of the Internship

The Microsoft Government Affairs Internship provides a unique opportunity to gain practical experience in international technology policy and corporate public affairs.

Benefits include:

  • Experience working at Microsoft
  • Exposure to European policymaking
  • Networking with EU stakeholders and policymakers
  • Participation in high-level political engagements
  • Professional mentorship
  • International work environment
  • Hands-on public affairs experience
  • Opportunity to contribute to technology governance discussions
  • Career development in government affairs and policy strategy

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This internship is particularly valuable because it combines:

  • Technology policy
  • International governance
  • Public affairs
  • Corporate strategy
  • Political engagement
  • European Union policymaking

Interns will work at the intersection of politics, business, and technology during a period of rapid transformation in Europe’s digital landscape.

For students and graduates interested in global policy careers, digital governance, AI regulation, or international public affairs, this internship offers world-class exposure and professional growth opportunities.

Application Deadline

Microsoft stated that:

  • The position will remain open for a minimum of 5 days.
  • Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis until the position is filled.

Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible.

Final Thoughts

The Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 in Brussels present a remarkable opportunity for ambitious young professionals seeking careers in international policy, corporate affairs, digital governance, and European technology regulation.

By joining Microsoft’s European Government Affairs team, interns will gain direct experience shaping conversations around cloud sovereignty, AI infrastructure, telecom policy, and Europe’s evolving digital future.

For candidates passionate about technology, politics, innovation, and global impact, this internship could become a major stepping stone toward an international policy career.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Microsoft Government Affairs Internship Opportunities 2026 in Brussels, Belgium | 12-Month European Policy Internship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Yale University Scholarships 2026-27(Fully-funded for Undergraduate, Masters and PhD)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Start your study adventure in USA. Good news! Yale University Scholarships are currently open. In this article we will explain in detail about this scholarship, its benefits and step by step application process. Yale University Scholarship is a fully funded scholarship for international students. This scholarship is offered for undergraduate, masters and PhD. Yale Scholarship […]

The post Yale University Scholarships 2026-27(Fully-funded for Undergraduate, Masters and PhD) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Start your study adventure in USA. Good news! Yale University Scholarships are currently open. In this article we will explain in detail about this scholarship, its benefits and step by step application process.

Yale University Scholarship is a fully funded scholarship for international students. This scholarship is offered for undergraduate, masters and PhD. Yale Scholarship can vary from a few hundred dollars to over $70,000 per year; the average Yale need-based scholarship is over $50,000.

This university is one of the greatest universities in the USA and is part of the Ivy League schools. The Ivy League schools are synonymous with prestige and academic excellence. The dream of many high school students is to study at Ivy League schools because professional success is almost guaranteed by studying at these universities.

Yale University is the third oldest university in the US and was found in 1701 at New Haven, Connecticut. It was originally founded as a school to educate the clergy but soon was changed to teach many other fields of study. Many well-known students have graduated from Yale including Nobel Laureates, Supreme Court Justices, US Presidents and Rhodes Scholars.

This university is ranked among the top universities in the world because of its academic brilliance. This university stands at 15th in the world by top Universities and 3rd in the US by the US News because of its prestige and an excellent academic standard.

Yale University offer a number of scholarships in the form of financial aid. Approximately 63% of undergraduate students, including international, received financial aid. For graduate scholarships, students have to apply to the departments of their choice because funding policies are different for each department. Graduate funding is determined by individual departments, not the university.

For PhD. students, fully funded scholarships are very common. Every student at least receive scholarship which covers full tuition scholarship, hospitalization fellowship, and an annual stipend.

Scholarship Summary

  • Level of Study: Undergraduate, Masters, PhD
  • Institution(s): Yale University
  • Study in: USA
  • Opportunity Focus Areas:
  • To view the full list of undergraduate majors click here.
  • To view the full list of graduate majors click here.
  • Program Period: Program period depends on the chosen Program from 2 to 5 Academic Years.
  • Deadline: Click here for undergraduate deadlines / Click here for graduate deadlines

Scholarship Coverage

  • Yale University scholarship is awarded to cover 100% of the financial need demonstrated by the family.
  • They take into account your parent’s income, assets, and finances to determine your scholarship amount.
  • The Yale Scholarship can vary from a few hundred dollars to over $70,000 per year; the average Yale need-based scholarship is over $50,000.
  • Also, students whose parents earn less than 65,000$ a year do not pay anything! So if your parents earn less than 65,000$ a year, Yale University scholarship covers tuition, room, and board!

 

Eligibility Criteria for Yale University Scholarships

To be eligible for Yale University Scholarships, following criteria is to be fulfilled:

  • Required Language: English.
    • TOEFL scores of at least the following:
      • 100 on the internet-based TOEFL
      • 600 on the paper-based TOEFL
      • 250 on the computer-based TOEFL
    • IELTS scores of 7 or higher and Pearson Test scores of 70 or higher.
      • For more information, check here.
  • Eligible Countries: All World Countries.
  • You are enrolled in or eligible to participate in the Federal Free or Reduced Price Lunch program (FRPL).
  • You have received or are eligible to receive an ACT or SAT testing fee waiver.
  • You are enrolled in a federal, state, or local program that aids students from low-income families (e.g., TRIO programs such as Upward Bound).
  • You are a ward of the state or an orphan.
  • Your annual family income falls within the Income Eligibility Guidelines set by the USDA Food and Nutrition Service.
  • You live in a federally subsidized public housing, a foster home or are homeless.
  • Your family receives public assistance.
  • You can provide a supporting statement from a school official, college access counselor, financial aid officer, or community leader.

 

How to Apply for Yale University Scholarship?

Please follow the following steps to avail Yale University Scholarship:

  1. Check these specific requirements of this scholarship:
    • Two Teacher Recommendations
    • $80 Application Fee or Fee Waiver
    • Standardized Testing Results
    • Counselor Recommendation and School Report (including transcript)
    • Final Report
    • Mid-Year Report
  2. For Undergraduate students, click here to apply.
  3. For graduate students. Please apply through choosing the appropriate program from here.
  4. You will get an e-mail for acceptance or refuse.

Website

To know more about Yale University Scholarship, please visit official website:

Official Website

The post Yale University Scholarships 2026-27(Fully-funded for Undergraduate, Masters and PhD) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate Position 2026: Apply for a Three-Year Research, Education, and Training Role at MIT
item.dc:creator: Hildah W. Nakafu
item.description:
The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab, widely known as J-PAL, is inviting applications for the Senior Training Associate position at J-PAL Global. This opportunity is based at the J-PAL Global office at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States. This is a strong opportunity for candidates with a background in economics, […]

The post J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate Position 2026: Apply for a Three-Year Research, Education, and Training Role at MIT appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab, widely known as J-PAL, is inviting applications for the Senior Training Associate position at J-PAL Global. This opportunity is based at the J-PAL Global office at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

This is a strong opportunity for candidates with a background in economics, political science, education, international development, program evaluation, quantitative research, randomized evaluations, or related fields. The role is designed for individuals who are passionate about poverty alleviation, evidence-informed policymaking, rigorous impact evaluation, and global development research.

The successful candidate will join J-PAL’s Research, Education, and Training team, supporting the design, planning, delivery, and evaluation of training programs for policymakers, practitioners, researchers, governments, NGOs, foundations, universities, and development organizations around the world.

About J-PAL Global

The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab is a global research center working to reduce poverty by ensuring that public policy is shaped by scientific evidence. J-PAL is anchored by a network of more than 1,100 leading researchers based at universities across the world.

Headquartered at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, J-PAL works across multiple global offices and has more than 500 staff members supporting research, policy engagement, education, training, and partnerships.

J-PAL’s work focuses on using rigorous research, especially randomized evaluations, to understand what works in reducing poverty and improving lives. Through its partnerships with governments, non-governmental organizations, foundations, multilateral institutions, and universities, J-PAL helps turn evidence into policy action.

Position Overview

J-PAL Global is recruiting a Senior Training Associate to support its mission of promoting evidence-informed policy. The position will be based at the J-PAL Global office at MIT in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

The role is part of J-PAL’s Research, Education, and Training team, also known as the RET team. This team works in a fast-paced and collaborative environment to equip policymakers, researchers, practitioners, and staff with the knowledge and tools needed to conduct and use rigorous impact evaluations.

The Senior Training Associate will contribute to J-PAL’s global training work by developing new teaching materials, supporting online and in-person courses, assisting with workshops, and strengthening learning resources on randomized evaluations and cost-effectiveness.

The role also includes working with learners and partners from around the world, creating open-access resources for J-PAL’s online courses, and developing tailored training materials for partner organizations.

Key Details of the Opportunity

  • Position title: Senior Training Associate
  • Organization: J-PAL Global
  • Host institution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
  • Location: Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States
  • Earliest start date: 1 July 2026
  • Length of commitment: Three years or more
  • Application deadline: June 15
  • Application review: Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis
  • Work format: Hybrid, with at least two in-office days per week
  • Visa sponsorship: Not available

Why This Role Matters

This position is ideal for someone who wants to contribute to global development through research, training, and evidence-based policy. Many governments and development organizations want to solve urgent problems related to poverty, education, health, labor markets, social protection, agriculture, governance, and economic opportunity.

However, effective solutions require strong evidence. J-PAL’s training work helps policymakers, researchers, and development practitioners understand how to generate, interpret, and use evidence responsibly.

As a Senior Training Associate, the selected candidate will help build the capacity of people and institutions working on real-world policy challenges. The role combines research communication, teaching, data analysis, training design, partnership support, and global development engagement.

Main Responsibilities

The Senior Training Associate will contribute to several areas of J-PAL’s training and education work. Responsibilities may include the following:

1. Developing Teaching Materials

The selected candidate will research and design new teaching modules for J-PAL’s training programs. These materials may cover topics such as randomized evaluations, impact evaluation methods, cost-effectiveness analysis, data use, and evidence-informed decision-making.

This may involve preparing:

  • Presentations
  • Case studies
  • Training manuals
  • Course exercises
  • Learning guides
  • Technical explanations
  • Teaching resources for online and in-person learners

2. Supporting Online and In-Person Training

The Senior Training Associate will serve as a teaching assistant for J-PAL’s online and in-person training offerings. This includes helping learners strengthen their ability to generate and use rigorous evidence.

The role may involve supporting learners during courses, explaining technical concepts, assisting with assignments, answering questions, and helping participants understand randomized evaluation methods.

3. Planning and Delivering Workshops

The position involves assisting with the planning, organization, and delivery of training courses and workshops for key partners around the world.

These partners may include:

  • Government agencies
  • Non-governmental organizations
  • Foundations
  • Universities
  • Multilateral aid agencies
  • Research institutions
  • Development practitioners

The selected candidate may also have opportunities to travel domestically and internationally to support or participate in training courses.

4. Collaborating Across J-PAL Offices

J-PAL operates through a global network of regional offices. The Senior Training Associate will coordinate and collaborate with training colleagues across these offices.

This may include contributing to knowledge management, improving training processes, organizing shared resources, and helping ensure consistency and quality across J-PAL’s training materials.

5. Monitoring, Evaluation, and Data Analysis

The role includes maintaining tracking, monitoring, and evaluation systems for J-PAL’s courses. The selected candidate will help analyze data to assess learning outcomes, participant engagement, and course effectiveness.

This makes the role suitable for candidates who are comfortable with quantitative data analysis and interested in improving educational and training programs through evidence.

6. Supporting Partner Relationships

The Senior Training Associate will also support the development of relationships with key partners in governments, NGOs, foundations, universities, multilateral aid agencies, and other institutions around the world.

This aspect of the role requires strong communication skills, cultural awareness, professionalism, and an interest in working across different institutional and global contexts.

Required Qualifications

Applicants should meet the following educational, technical, and professional requirements.

Educational Background

A bachelor’s degree is required in one of the following fields:

  • Economics
  • Political science
  • Education
  • International development
  • A related field

A master’s degree is preferred, but not strictly required.

Applicants must have training in and a strong technical understanding of:

  • Econometrics
  • Randomized evaluations
  • Quantitative research methods
  • Impact evaluation methods

Experience Requirements

J-PAL prefers candidates with around four years of relevant work experience. This experience may include work gained as a student.

Relevant experience may include:

  • Program evaluation
  • Quantitative data analysis
  • Economics research
  • Development research
  • Education or training work
  • Impact evaluation
  • Policy research
  • Research assistance
  • Data management
  • Related analytical roles

Applicants with fewer than four years of experience are also welcome to apply. Exceptional candidates with less than four years of experience may be considered for an Associate-level position.

Skills and Competencies Required

Strong Interest in Poverty Alleviation

Candidates should have a strong interest in poverty alleviation policy and research. This interest may be demonstrated through coursework, research, study abroad, work abroad, development experience, or professional exposure to policy and social impact work.

Quantitative Skills

Applicants must have knowledge of:

  • Data management
  • Data analysis
  • Econometrics
  • Quantitative research methods

These skills are important because the role involves working with training materials related to randomized evaluations, evidence generation, and policy-relevant research.

Software Skills

Applicants must have a good command of at least one of the following:

  • Stata
  • R
  • Python

Candidates should also be comfortable using Google Workspace tools, including:

  • Google Docs
  • Google Sheets
  • Google Slides
  • Google Forms

Additional skills that are considered a plus include:

  • LaTeX
  • GitHub
  • Other coding languages
  • Thoughtful use of AI tools to streamline workflows

Cultural Competencies

J-PAL is looking for applicants who are comfortable working in a diverse and multicultural environment. The role involves collaboration with colleagues, learners, partners, policymakers, and institutions from different parts of the world.

Applicants should demonstrate sensitivity and adaptability across:

  • Culture
  • Gender
  • Religion
  • Race
  • Nationality
  • Age
  • Professional background

J-PAL encourages diversity, equity, and inclusion in its work and welcomes applicants from all backgrounds. The organization especially encourages people who are underrepresented in economics to apply.

Location and Work Arrangement

This position is based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, at J-PAL Global’s office at MIT.

The role follows a hybrid work arrangement. Staff are currently expected to work in the office at least two days per week, specifically Tuesdays and Wednesdays. Remote work is allowed on the remaining three days.

However, hybrid work policies may change annually. The position is not eligible for full-time remote work.

Visa Sponsorship

Visa sponsorship is not available for this position. Candidates must already be authorized to work in the United States for a minimum of three years.

This is an important requirement, and applicants should confirm their work authorization before applying.

Compensation and Benefits

J-PAL states that pay range and pay grade information for this role is available through the relevant MIT compensation information page.

As a member of the MIT community, the successful candidate will receive a competitive compensation package for the non-profit international development field.

Benefits may include:

  • Comprehensive health insurance
  • 401(k) match
  • Pension-based retirement plan
  • Tuition assistance
  • Commuter benefits
  • MIT community discounts
  • Generous vacation policy
  • Additional MIT benefits

J-PAL staff members may also be eligible to take proctored exams in the Data, Economics, and Design of Policy MicroMasters courses at no cost. These course certificates can count toward the DEDP MicroMasters credential.

Application Process

Interested candidates must apply through the MIT human resources system.

Applicants should search for MIT Job ID: 25712 and complete the application on the MIT portal.

The application requires candidates to upload a single PDF document into the “Resume” field. This single PDF should include:

  1. Cover letter
    The cover letter should explain why the applicant is interested in the position at J-PAL Global and why they are qualified for the role.
  2. Resume or CV
    The resume or CV should clearly show relevant education, work experience, technical skills, research experience, and training-related experience.
  3. Transcripts for all previous university-level coursework
    Unofficial transcripts are accepted.

Applicants should only apply to the J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate position, MIT Job ID 25712.

Selection Process

All applicants will be asked to complete a short data task as part of the screening process.

Shortlisted applicants will then be asked to complete a short technical screening interview via Zoom.

Candidates who advance further will be invited to additional Zoom interviews.

Because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible before the June 15 deadline.

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity is suitable for candidates who want to combine research, education, training, and global development impact. It is especially relevant for people who enjoy translating technical research concepts into practical learning materials for policymakers, practitioners, and researchers.

You should consider applying if you:

  • Are passionate about poverty reduction and evidence-based policy
  • Have a background in economics, education, political science, international development, or related fields
  • Understand econometrics, randomized evaluations, or quantitative research methods
  • Enjoy developing teaching materials and training resources
  • Have experience in data analysis or program evaluation
  • Want to work with global partners in government, NGOs, foundations, universities, and multilateral agencies
  • Are comfortable working in a multicultural environment
  • Are authorized to work in the United States for at least three years

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate position is June 15.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so applicants should submit their materials as early as possible.

How to Apply

To apply, candidates should visit the MIT human resources system, search for MIT Job ID 25712, and submit the required application materials.

Applicants must upload one PDF document containing their cover letter, resume or CV, and transcripts.

APPLY HERE.

For more information about this opportunity click here.

Discover more job opportunities on OFY here.

The post J-PAL Global Senior Training Associate Position 2026: Apply for a Three-Year Research, Education, and Training Role at MIT appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The International Criminal Court (ICC) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme 2026 sponsored by the Republic of Korea. This highly competitive international opportunity offers young Korean professionals the chance to work at the ICC headquarters in The Hague, Netherlands, while contributing to global […]

The post ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Criminal Court (ICC) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme 2026 sponsored by the Republic of Korea. This highly competitive international opportunity offers young Korean professionals the chance to work at the ICC headquarters in The Hague, Netherlands, while contributing to global justice and international institutional management.

The position is hosted within the Human Resources Section of the ICC Registry and provides selected candidates with hands-on experience in strategic HR management, organizational development, policy implementation, workforce planning, recruitment operations, learning and development, and international administration.

The application deadline is 26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time).

About the International Criminal Court (ICC)

The International Criminal Court is one of the world’s leading international judicial institutions, responsible for investigating and prosecuting individuals accused of genocide, crimes against humanity, war crimes, and crimes of aggression.

Headquartered in The Hague, Netherlands, the ICC operates with professionals from diverse cultural and professional backgrounds. The organization provides an international working environment focused on justice, accountability, professionalism, diversity, and global cooperation.

The Human Resources Section plays a critical role in supporting the Court’s global mission by ensuring effective workforce management, organizational efficiency, strategic talent development, and compliance with international administrative standards.

Overview of the JPO Position

Position Title

Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – Junior Professional Officer (JPO)

Duty Station

The Hague, Netherlands

Organization

International Criminal Court (ICC)

Organizational Unit

Human Resources Section, Division of Management Services, Registry

Contract Duration

One-year appointment with possibility of extension according to the JPO Memorandum of Understanding

Annual Salary

Minimum Net Annual Salary (including post adjustment): €71,173

Application Deadline

26 June 2026 (midnight The Hague time)

What Makes This Opportunity Significant?

This JPO role offers early-career professionals the rare opportunity to:

  • Work directly within a major international organization
  • Gain exposure to UN Common System HR frameworks
  • Participate in strategic HR and organizational development projects
  • Build expertise in international recruitment and policy implementation
  • Contribute to organizational transformation initiatives
  • Develop experience in multicultural and multinational workplace management
  • Work alongside global experts in administration, HR operations, and institutional governance

For candidates interested in long-term careers with the United Nations system, international NGOs, global institutions, or diplomatic organizations, this opportunity provides outstanding career-building exposure.

Human Resources Section at the ICC

The Human Resources Section provides centralized HR advisory, administrative, and strategic support across the Court. It operates within the Division of Management Services alongside:

  • Budget Section
  • Finance Section
  • Safety and Security Section
  • General Services Section
  • Occupational Health Unit
  • SAP Competence Centre

The HR Section focuses on both operational and strategic human resource management functions.

Key Areas Managed by the HR Section

The section oversees:

  • Recruitment and onboarding
  • Workforce planning
  • Job design and classification
  • Payroll administration
  • Employee benefits and entitlements
  • Pension and insurance management
  • Performance management
  • Organizational development
  • Learning and development programmes
  • Career planning and succession management
  • Staff relations and grievance support

Structure of the Human Resources Section

The HR Section consists of two specialized units:

1. HR Operations Unit

This unit handles operational HR functions including:

  • Recruitment processes
  • Staff onboarding
  • Compensation administration
  • Payroll management
  • Benefits and entitlements
  • Post management
  • Health insurance administration
  • Pension administration
  • HR-related staff complaints and grievances
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) staff data management

2. HR Organizational Development Unit

This unit focuses on strategic HR initiatives such as:

  • Workforce planning
  • Talent management
  • Organizational design
  • Staff development
  • Training programmes
  • Learning and development strategies
  • Performance management systems
  • Leadership and managerial development

Duties and Responsibilities

Under the supervision of the Head of the Human Resources Operations Unit, the selected JPO will support multiple HR functions and strategic projects.

Main Responsibilities Include:

  1. Supporting implementation of HR-related projects and strategic initiatives
  2. Conducting research on HR policies, procedures, and best practices from comparable international organizations
  3. Assisting in the development of HR guidelines and institutional procedures
  4. Liaising with HR specialists on recruitment, entitlements, insurance, policy interpretation, and administrative matters
  5. Reviewing HR issues and presenting analytical recommendations to management
  6. Supporting the monitoring and evaluation of HR policies and organizational practices
  7. Assisting in the development of HR performance indicators and management tools
  8. Contributing to Service Level Agreements and HR work plan tracking systems
  9. Conducting preliminary policy analysis and assisting in drafting position papers
  10. Designing internal communication materials supporting HR objectives and institutional goals
  11. Providing support across HR units whenever required
  12. Performing additional duties assigned by HR leadership

Required Qualifications

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess either:

  • An advanced university degree in:
    • Human Resources Management
    • Business Administration
    • Public Administration
    • Law
    • Social Sciences
    • Information Management
    • Or another related field

OR

  • A first-level university degree combined with two additional years of qualifying experience

Professional Experience Requirements

Candidates must have:

  • Minimum two years of relevant HR experience

OR

  • Four years of experience if applying with a first-level university degree

Desirable Experience

Additional advantages include experience in:

  • International organizations
  • UN Common System salary and allowance structures
  • Broad HR operations and administration
  • HR policy implementation
  • Organizational development initiatives

Required Skills and Competencies

The ICC seeks candidates with strong technical and interpersonal competencies.

Important Skills Include:

  • Human resources management knowledge
  • Strong analytical and research skills
  • Strategic planning capabilities
  • Results-oriented work approach
  • Ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines
  • Excellent communication skills
  • Professional writing ability
  • Cross-cultural collaboration skills
  • Confidentiality and discretion
  • Flexibility and motivation
  • Strong professional integrity

Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Fluency in English or French

Knowledge of the second working language is considered desirable.

Additional knowledge of official ICC languages such as:

  • Arabic
  • Chinese
  • Russian
  • Spanish

will be considered an advantage.

Eligibility Requirements for Republic of Korea JPO Applicants

This position is specifically sponsored by the Republic of Korea and therefore includes additional eligibility conditions.

Applicants must:

  • Be Korean citizens
  • Be fluent in Korean and English
  • Hold at least a Bachelor’s degree
  • Meet English proficiency requirements
  • Be under 32 years old as of 31 December 2026
  • Have completed or be exempt from Korean military service (for male applicants)

Accepted English Test Scores

Applicants must provide valid English test results meeting at least one of the following minimum scores:

  • TOEFL iBT: 100
  • TEPS: 430
  • IELTS: 7
  • TOEIC: 900

Additional notes:

  • TOEFL Home Edition is accepted
  • TOEFL MyBest Scores are not accepted
  • IELTS Academic and General Training are both accepted
  • Scores remain valid for two years after the test date

Military Service Age Extension

The maximum age limit may be extended for applicants who completed Korean military service:

  • One-year extension for service under one year
  • Two-year extension for service between one and two years
  • Three-year extension for service of two years or more

Required Documents

Applicants must submit all documents in English through the ICC e-recruitment system.

Required Documents Include:

  1. Resident Register issued by the Korean Government
  2. University degree certificates
  3. English proficiency certificate
  4. Employment certificates detailing:
    • Employment period
    • Organization
    • Department
    • Position title
    • Duties and responsibilities
  5. Military service certificate (if applicable)
  6. National Basic Livelihood Security certificate (for low-income applicants)
  7. Disability certificate (for applicants with disabilities)

Why You Should Apply

This ICC JPO opportunity offers substantial professional and international career benefits, including:

  • International HR experience
  • Exposure to global institutional operations
  • High-level policy and organizational development work
  • Competitive international salary
  • Multicultural professional environment
  • Networking with global professionals
  • Career pathway into international organizations and the UN system
  • Professional growth in strategic HR management

Final Thoughts

The ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) JPO Programme 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious Korean professionals seeking international exposure in human resources management and organizational development.

Working at the International Criminal Court provides not only a competitive international career pathway but also the opportunity to contribute to one of the world’s most significant global justice institutions.

Candidates with strong HR expertise, analytical capabilities, international outlook, and commitment to public service are strongly encouraged to apply before the 26 June 2026 deadline.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the ICC Associate Human Resources Officer job opportunity.

Explore more global job opportunities on OFY here.

The post ICC Associate Human Resources Officer (P-2) – JPO 2026 in The Hague, Netherlands | Salary €71,173 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 by Bombshell Impact Design Agency, an award-winning social impact branding and design agency recognized as the Best Social Impact Branding & Design Agency 2026 by the Corporate Vision Canadian Business Awards. This global initiative is designed […]

The post Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 by Bombshell Impact Design Agency, an award-winning social impact branding and design agency recognized as the Best Social Impact Branding & Design Agency 2026 by the Corporate Vision Canadian Business Awards.

This global initiative is designed to empower social impact organizations that are creating meaningful change in communities around the world. Through this grant, selected organizations will receive up to $10,000 USD worth of professional design, marketing, branding, and AI-powered business support services to help amplify their mission and increase visibility.

The application deadline is August 19, 2026, in recognition of World Humanitarian Day.

About the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant

The “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant is more than a funding opportunity. It is a visibility and growth initiative created specifically for organizations that are driving social transformation but may lack the branding, communication, or digital infrastructure needed to expand their reach and impact.

Bombshell Impact Design Agency believes that many world-changing missions remain unseen not because of lack of value, but because of limited visibility, storytelling, and strategic positioning. This initiative aims to bridge that gap by helping impactful organizations strengthen how they communicate and present their mission to the world.

The program welcomes applications from organizations across every region globally, reinforcing the agency’s commitment to supporting changemakers regardless of geographic location.

Grant Benefits

Selected recipients can receive up to $10,000 USD in professional services, including:

  • Branding and identity development
  • Strategic marketing support
  • AI-powered creative and communication services
  • Digital visibility enhancement
  • Storytelling and campaign support
  • Design solutions tailored to mission-driven organizations
  • Strategic impact communication support

These services are intended to help organizations improve outreach, attract supporters, strengthen campaigns, and scale their impact more effectively.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Many social impact organizations struggle with limited resources for branding, communication, and digital marketing. While their missions may be transformative, they often face challenges in:

  • Building public awareness
  • Reaching donors and supporters
  • Creating compelling digital campaigns
  • Developing strong visual identities
  • Competing for visibility online
  • Communicating measurable impact effectively

This grant directly addresses those challenges by providing expert-level creative and strategic services that many organizations would otherwise struggle to afford.

Who Can Apply?

The opportunity is open to social impact organizations worldwide that are actively working to create positive change in areas such as:

  • Sustainability
  • Environmental protection
  • Community development
  • Humanitarian support
  • Education
  • Accessibility
  • Social justice
  • Health and wellbeing
  • Youth empowerment
  • Innovation for social good

Organizations of different sizes and stages are encouraged to apply, especially those with compelling missions and measurable impact stories.

How to Apply

Applicants must complete a simple multi-step application process.

Step 1: Complete the Application Form

Organizations are required to provide information about:

  • Their mission
  • Their organization
  • The impact they are creating
  • Their long-term goals
  • The communities they serve

Applicants should clearly explain how the grant support would help expand or strengthen their work.

Step 2: Upload a Short Video

Applicants must submit a 30–60 second video filmed on a phone or simple camera setup.

The video should:

  • Introduce the organization
  • Share the mission and impact story
  • Explain why the work matters
  • Highlight the organization’s vision for change

The agency specifically encourages applicants to share their story authentically and in their own voice.

Step 3: Application Review and Winner Selection

After the deadline closes, all applications will be reviewed carefully by the Bombshell Impact Design Agency team. Selected winners will then be announced following the evaluation process.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: August 19, 2026

The deadline coincides with World Humanitarian Day, emphasizing the global importance of humanitarian and social impact initiatives.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and prepare a compelling story-driven application.

A Global Opportunity for Changemakers

One of the strongest aspects of this initiative is its international accessibility. Organizations from every continent are eligible to apply, making this a truly global social impact opportunity.

Whether an organization is based in Africa, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Oceania, or the Middle East, they are encouraged to submit their application if they are making meaningful contributions toward positive change.

The Core Message Behind the Initiative

The campaign carries a powerful message:

“The sky was never the limit. Visibility was.”

This reflects the idea that many organizations already possess the passion, innovation, and commitment needed to change the world. What they often need most is strategic visibility and stronger communication support to reach larger audiences and unlock greater opportunities.

Why Organizations Should Apply

This grant presents a unique opportunity for social impact organizations to gain access to high-value professional services without direct financial cost.

Organizations should consider applying because it offers:

  • International visibility
  • Access to expert branding support
  • Professional marketing services
  • AI-driven creative solutions
  • Increased awareness opportunities
  • Stronger communication tools
  • Enhanced storytelling capacity
  • Strategic mission amplification

For organizations seeking growth, awareness, and stronger public engagement, this initiative could become a major catalyst for expansion and long-term impact.

Final Thoughts

The Bombshell Impact Design Agency Social Impact Grant 2026 stands out as an innovative and mission-driven opportunity for organizations working toward meaningful global change.

Rather than focusing solely on financial funding, the initiative addresses one of the biggest modern challenges facing nonprofits and social enterprises: visibility.

For organizations with transformative missions but limited branding or marketing capacity, this grant could provide the professional support needed to elevate their message, strengthen their outreach, and expand their influence globally.

Interested organizations should begin preparing their applications, impact narratives, and short video submissions before the August 19, 2026 deadline.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Bombshell Impact Design Agency Launches the 2nd Annual “The Sky Is Not The Limit” Social Impact Grant 2026 — Win Up to $10,000 in Design, Marketing & AI Services appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026: Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates in Switzerland, China, India, and Japan
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications Open for the World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026 Key Details Organization: World Economic Forum (WEF) Programme: Early Careers Programme – Fall 2026 Cohort Locations: Geneva (Switzerland), Beijing (China), Mumbai (India), Tokyo (Japan) Duration: 6 months (full-time, onsite) Start Date: Between September 1 and October 1, 2026 Eligibility: Bachelor’s or Master’s graduates with […]

The post World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026: Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates in Switzerland, China, India, and Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Open for the World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026

Key Details

  • Organization: World Economic Forum (WEF)
  • Programme: Early Careers Programme – Fall 2026 Cohort
  • Locations: Geneva (Switzerland), Beijing (China), Mumbai (India), Tokyo (Japan)
  • Duration: 6 months (full-time, onsite)
  • Start Date: Between September 1 and October 1, 2026
  • Eligibility: Bachelor’s or Master’s graduates with up to 3 years of cumulative work experience (including internships)
  • Monthly Compensation:
    • Geneva: CHF 4,500
    • Beijing: CNY 12,500
    • Mumbai: INR 91,666
    • Tokyo: JPY 333,333
  • Work Authorization Support: Available for Geneva-based positions only
  • Application Deadline: June 14, 2026

Are you a recent graduate looking to launch your international career with one of the world’s leading organizations? The World Economic Forum (WEF) Early Careers Programme 2026 is now accepting applications for its Fall 2026 cohort.
