This paid six-month programme offers young professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience while contributing to solutions for some of the world’s most pressing global challenges.

Successful candidates will be based in Geneva (Switzerland), Beijing (China), Mumbai (India), or Tokyo (Japan) and will work alongside experts, policymakers, business leaders, and innovators from around the world.

About the World Economic Forum

The World Economic Forum is an independent international organization committed to improving the state of the world through public-private cooperation. Headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, the Forum brings together leaders from government, business, academia, civil society, and international organizations to address global issues and drive collective action.

With over 1,000 employees representing more than 100 nationalities, the Forum provides a dynamic and multicultural working environment where young professionals can develop valuable skills and expand their global networks.

Programme Details

Duration

  • 6-month full-time onsite employment

Start Date

  • Between September 1 and October 1, 2026

Locations

  • Geneva, Switzerland
  • Beijing, China
  • Mumbai, India
  • Tokyo, Japan

Compensation

Participants will receive competitive monthly compensation:

  • Geneva: CHF 4,500 (salary and medical allowance)
  • Beijing: CNY 12,500
  • Mumbai: INR 91,666
  • Tokyo: JPY 333,333

Work Authorization

Please note that visa and work authorization support is available only for Geneva-based positions.

Who Can Apply?

The programme is designed for:

  • Bachelor’s degree graduates
  • Master’s degree graduates
  • Candidates with a maximum of three years of cumulative work experience, including internships

This opportunity is ideal for recent graduates seeking international exposure and practical experience within a globally recognized organization.

Available Career Areas

Participants can join various functional and thematic teams across the organization.

Business Development and Engagement

Support strategic partnerships and stakeholder engagement initiatives that advance the Forum’s mission.

Project and Community Management

Coordinate collaborative projects and help global communities drive progress on critical challenges.

Applicants interested in these areas can select from thematic sectors including:

  • Advanced Manufacturing and Supply Chains
  • Artificial Intelligence, Frontier Technologies, and Cybersecurity
  • Economic Growth, Equity, Inclusion, Human Capital, and Skills
  • Energy and Materials
  • Financial and Monetary Systems
  • Regional Government Agenda, Trade, and Geopolitics
  • Health and Healthcare
  • Sustainability, Nature, and Climate
  • Urban Transformation

Digital and Data

Support the digital infrastructure, data systems, and technological tools that enable global collaboration.

Events and Experience

Help design and deliver world-class conferences, meetings, and digital experiences.

Communication and Outreach

Create engaging content and communications that showcase the Forum’s initiatives and impact.

Core Enabling Functions

Support the organization’s operations through roles in:

  • Finance, Procurement, Legal and Compliance
  • Hospitality and Facility Management
  • People and Culture (Human Resources)

Skills You Will Develop

Participants will gain valuable professional competencies, including:

  • Global stakeholder engagement
  • Relationship management
  • Strategic communication
  • Problem-solving and critical thinking
  • Project management
  • Adaptability and resilience
  • Professional judgment and decision-making
  • Cross-cultural collaboration

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the Fall 2026 cohort.

Selection Process

The recruitment process includes several stages:

  1. Application submission
  2. Initial screening
  3. Asynchronous video interview
  4. Interviews with hiring managers and team members
  5. Personality assessment
  6. Talent Acquisition interview
  7. Reference and background checks
  8. Final offer

Why Apply?

The World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Gain international work experience
  • Build a global professional network
  • Work on projects with real-world impact
  • Develop highly sought-after professional skills
  • Collaborate with leaders from government, business, and civil society
  • Enhance your career prospects in international development, public policy, business, technology, sustainability, and global affairs

For graduates passionate about shaping the future and addressing global challenges, this programme provides an exceptional platform to learn, contribute, and grow.

Application Deadline

Deadline: June 14, 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the Fall 2026 cohort.

 

The post World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026: Paid Global Opportunity for Recent Graduates in Switzerland, China, India, and Japan appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026 (USA) – Paid Opportunity for Recent Graduates
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Key Details Organization: World Economic Forum (WEF) Programme: Early Careers Programme – Fall 2026 Cohort (USA) Locations: New York and San Francisco, USA Duration: 6 months (full-time, onsite) Start Date: Between September 1 and October 1, 2026 Eligibility: Bachelor’s or Master’s graduates with up to 3 years of cumulative work experience (including internships) Compensation: USD […]

The post World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026 (USA) – Paid Opportunity for Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

  • Organization: World Economic Forum (WEF)
  • Programme: Early Careers Programme – Fall 2026 Cohort (USA)
  • Locations: New York and San Francisco, USA
  • Duration: 6 months (full-time, onsite)
  • Start Date: Between September 1 and October 1, 2026
  • Eligibility: Bachelor’s or Master’s graduates with up to 3 years of cumulative work experience (including internships)
  • Compensation: USD $4,550 per month
  • Application Deadline: June 14, 2026

Applications Open for the World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026 (USA)

The World Economic Forum (WEF) is now accepting applications for its Early Careers Programme 2026 (USA Cohort). This paid six-month programme offers recent graduates and early-career professionals the opportunity to gain practical experience while contributing to initiatives that address global challenges.

Successful candidates will be based in New York or San Francisco and work alongside leading experts, policymakers, business leaders, and innovators from around the world.

Areas of Opportunity

Participants may work in one of the following functional areas:

  • Business Development and Engagement
  • Project and Community Management
  • Digital and Data
  • Events and Experience
  • Communication and Outreach
  • Finance, Procurement, Legal and Compliance
  • Hospitality and Facility Management
  • People and Culture

Applicants interested in Business Development or Project and Community Management can choose to focus on:

  • Advanced Manufacturing and Supply Chains
  • Artificial Intelligence, Frontier Technologies and Cybersecurity
  • Economic Growth, Equity, Inclusion, Human Capital and Skills
  • Energy and Materials
  • Financial and Monetary Systems
  • Trade and Geopolitics
  • Health and Healthcare
  • Sustainability, Nature and Climate
  • Urban Transformation

What You Will Gain

Throughout the programme, participants will develop valuable skills in:

  • Stakeholder engagement and relationship management
  • Project coordination and community building
  • Strategic communication
  • Problem-solving and critical thinking
  • Cross-cultural collaboration
  • Professional judgment and decision-making
  • Adaptability and resilience

Interested candidates should submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the Fall 2026 cohort. CLICK HERE TO APPLY NOW

Selection Process

The recruitment process includes:

  1. Resume screening
  2. Asynchronous video interview
  3. Interviews with hiring managers and team members
  4. Occupational preferences questionnaire
  5. Talent Acquisition interview
  6. Reference and background checks

Why Apply?

The World Economic Forum offers a unique opportunity to gain experience within one of the world’s most influential international organizations. Participants will contribute to projects that drive public-private cooperation, expand their global networks, and develop skills that can accelerate careers across international development, business, policy, technology, sustainability, and social impact.

Application Deadline

Deadline: June 14, 2026

Interested candidates should submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the Fall 2026 cohort. CLICK HERE TO APPLY NOW

 

The post World Economic Forum Early Careers Programme 2026 (USA) – Paid Opportunity for Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
